prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase (no solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation (N−1) used charge +1 / multiplicity 2; anion (N+1) used charge −1 / multiplicity 2. Fukui indices were derived from finite-difference Mulliken partial charges across the neutral, N+1, and N−1 states. All three SCF calculations converged. Integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true); charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported. The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (most electrophilic site): atom 3 (C, the carbonyl carbon), f+ = 0.39938968. The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O, the carbonyl oxygen), f- = 0.23839906. Negative Fukui indices on hydrogen atoms are a known Mulliken artefact with large basis sets.
